title: Gate's pytest runs on SQLite — Postgres-only constraint violations (VARCHAR length, CHECK, UNIQUE) are invisible to the gate
description: config/settings/test.py runs the suite on in-memory SQLite, which does not enforce VARCHAR max_length, CHECK constraints, or DB-level UNIQUE the way the dev/production Postgres does. A class of bugs therefore passes `pytest` (the standing gate) on SQLite yet raises DataError / IntegrityError on Postgres in production: an ORM create/save writing author-controlled strings into a capped column, a CHECK-violating row, a UNIQUE collision. Surfaced by TASK-020/FGAP-008's _save_flag_requests, where a valid-JSON oversize flag-request value 500s on Postgres but no SQLite test could catch it; TASK-020's fix routes through full_clean() so the ValidationError path (which fires on BOTH DBs) is the safety net.

evidence.reference: test-suite DATABASES = in-memory SQLite (~:22-27); pytest selects this settings module

impact: The gate's pytest cannot catch Postgres-only constraint violations, so such bugs pass green and fail in production — a latent blind spot for any code path writing unvalidated input to constrained columns.
proposed_resolution: Mitigation already applied in TASK-020: full_clean() before saving author input + a test asserting the ValidationError path. Fuller fix is a scope call: run the gate's pytest against Postgres (full fidelity) or add a Postgres CI lane. User scopes.
